title: WAN Fundamentals
description: Practice identifying wide area network terminology, customer premises equipment (CPE), demarcation points, local loops, ISP tiers, and the fundamental concepts of WAN connectivity.
starterCode:
# WAN FUNDAMENTALS EXERCISE

## Part 1: WAN Terminology

1. WAN stands for _______ Area Network.
2. A WAN connects networks across large _______ distances, such as between cities, states, or countries.
3. The _______ loop is the physical connection between the customer premises and the service provider's central office.
4. A _______ office (CO) is the service provider's facility where local loops terminate.
5. WAN services are provided by telecommunications carriers also known as _______ providers.

## Part 2: Customer Premises Equipment

6. CPE stands for Customer _______ Equipment.
7. CPE includes any equipment located at the _______ site, such as routers, modems, and CSU/DSUs.
8. A _______ converts digital signals to analog for transmission over telephone lines.
9. The customer's _______ is the network device that connects the LAN to the WAN service.
10. CPE can be owned by the customer or _______ from the service provider.

## Part 3: Demarcation Point

11. The _______ point (demarc) is the boundary between the customer's network and the provider's network.
12. A _______ interface device (NID) is typically installed at the demarcation point.
13. The service provider is responsible for everything on _______ side of the demarc.
14. The customer is responsible for all equipment and cabling on _______ side of the demarc.
15. The demarc is usually located in a _______ closet or equipment room in commercial buildings.

## Part 4: ISP Tiers

16. Tier _______ ISPs own and operate large backbone networks that span entire continents or the globe.
17. Tier 1 ISPs connect to each other through _______ peering agreements with no payment exchanged.
18. Tier _______ ISPs purchase transit from Tier 1 providers and may also peer with other Tier 2 ISPs.
19. Tier 3 ISPs are typically _______ or regional providers that purchase transit from Tier 1 or Tier 2 ISPs.
20. An Internet _______ Point (IXP) is a physical location where ISPs exchange traffic between their networks.

## Part 5: WAN Connection Concepts

21. _______ is the measure of how much data can be transmitted over a WAN link per unit of time.
22. WAN _______ refers to the delay experienced when data travels across the wide area network.
23. A _______ -level agreement (SLA) defines the performance guarantees a provider commits to.
24. _______ is the variation in delay between packets traversing the WAN.
25. The last _______ refers to the final segment of the network connection to the end user's premises.

## Part 6: Scenario - WAN Design for Multi-Site Corporation

A corporation with headquarters in Chicago has branch offices in New York, Los Angeles, and Miami. All offices need reliable WAN connectivity for daily operations including voice, video, and data.

26. For connecting all four offices with direct links between every pair, the company would choose a full _______ topology.
27. The successor technology to Frame Relay that provides label-based forwarding in service provider networks is _______.
28. Compared to Internet VPN, MPLS offers more predictable performance but at a _______ cost.
29. To support 50 concurrent G.711 voice calls (each requiring 87 Kbps with overhead), the minimum WAN bandwidth needed is approximately _______ Mbps.
30. An SLA typically specifies maximum acceptable values for latency, jitter, and packet _______.

## Part 7: Scenario - WAN Failover Testing

During a disaster recovery test, the corporation's primary WAN link between Chicago and New York fails. The network team must ensure seamless failover.

31. Static routes with _______ objects can detect primary link failure and activate backup routes automatically.
32. IP _______ probes send periodic test packets to monitor WAN link health and trigger failover when thresholds are exceeded.
33. During failover to a lower-bandwidth backup link, _______ policies ensure critical traffic (voice, ERP) is prioritized over best-effort traffic.
34. WAN _______ techniques such as deduplication and compression reduce bandwidth consumption on backup links.
35. In SD-WAN architecture, the _______ network is the physical transport (MPLS, broadband) while the overlay is the virtual abstraction layer.
solution:
# SOLUTION

## Part 1: WAN Terminology

1. WAN stands for **Wide** Area Network.
2. A WAN connects networks across large **geographic** distances, such as between cities, states, or countries.
3. The **local** loop is the physical connection between the customer premises and the service provider's central office.
4. A **central** office (CO) is the service provider's facility where local loops terminate.
5. WAN services are provided by telecommunications carriers also known as **service** providers.

## Part 2: Customer Premises Equipment

6. CPE stands for Customer **Premises** Equipment.
7. CPE includes any equipment located at the **customer** site, such as routers, modems, and CSU/DSUs.
8. A **modem** converts digital signals to analog for transmission over telephone lines.
9. The customer's **router** is the network device that connects the LAN to the WAN service.
10. CPE can be owned by the customer or **leased** from the service provider.

## Part 3: Demarcation Point

11. The **demarcation** point (demarc) is the boundary between the customer's network and the provider's network.
12. A **network** interface device (NID) is typically installed at the demarcation point.
13. The service provider is responsible for everything on **their** side of the demarc.
14. The customer is responsible for all equipment and cabling on **their** side of the demarc.
15. The demarc is usually located in a **telecommunications** closet or equipment room in commercial buildings.

## Part 4: ISP Tiers

16. Tier **1** ISPs own and operate large backbone networks that span entire continents or the globe.
17. Tier 1 ISPs connect to each other through **settlement-free** peering agreements with no payment exchanged.
18. Tier **2** ISPs purchase transit from Tier 1 providers and may also peer with other Tier 2 ISPs.
19. Tier 3 ISPs are typically **local** or regional providers that purchase transit from Tier 1 or Tier 2 ISPs.
20. An Internet **Exchange** Point (IXP) is a physical location where ISPs exchange traffic between their networks.

## Part 5: WAN Connection Concepts

21. **Bandwidth** is the measure of how much data can be transmitted over a WAN link per unit of time.
22. WAN **latency** refers to the delay experienced when data travels across the wide area network.
23. A **service** -level agreement (SLA) defines the performance guarantees a provider commits to.
24. **Jitter** is the variation in delay between packets traversing the WAN.
25. The last **mile** refers to the final segment of the network connection to the end user's premises.

## Part 6: Scenario - WAN Design for Multi-Site Corporation

26. For connecting all four offices with direct links between every pair, the company would choose a full **mesh** topology.
27. The successor technology to Frame Relay that provides label-based forwarding in service provider networks is **MPLS**.
28. Compared to Internet VPN, MPLS offers more predictable performance but at a **higher** cost.
29. To support 50 concurrent G.711 voice calls (each requiring 87 Kbps with overhead), the minimum WAN bandwidth needed is approximately **4.35** Mbps.
30. An SLA typically specifies maximum acceptable values for latency, jitter, and packet **loss**.

## Part 7: Scenario - WAN Failover Testing

31. Static routes with **tracking** objects can detect primary link failure and activate backup routes automatically.
32. IP **SLA** probes send periodic test packets to monitor WAN link health and trigger failover when thresholds are exceeded.
33. During failover to a lower-bandwidth backup link, **QoS** policies ensure critical traffic (voice, ERP) is prioritized over best-effort traffic.
34. WAN **optimization** techniques such as deduplication and compression reduce bandwidth consumption on backup links.
35. In SD-WAN architecture, the **underlay** network is the physical transport (MPLS, broadband) while the overlay is the virtual abstraction layer.
hints: WAN (Wide Area Network) connects geographically distant LANs — think inter-city or international links.
The local loop is the physical cable from your premises to the provider's central office (CO).
CPE (Customer Premises Equipment) includes routers, modems, CSU/DSUs — anything at the customer site.
The demarcation point (demarc) is where provider responsibility ends and customer responsibility begins.
A NID (Network Interface Device) is the physical box at the demarc where the provider's line terminates.
Tier 1 ISPs are the backbone of the internet — they peer with each other settlement-free (no payment).
Tier 2 ISPs buy transit from Tier 1 and may peer with other Tier 2s; Tier 3 are local/regional providers.
IXPs (Internet Exchange Points) are neutral facilities where ISPs interconnect to exchange traffic.
Jitter is the variation in packet delay — critical for voice and video applications.
The last mile is often the bottleneck — it's the final connection segment to the customer.

testCases.description: Should correctly explain mentions jitter concepts and implementation details
Should correctly explain identifies modem concepts and implementation details
